chapter_title: About Continuing to do Good Deeds

hadith_entries.text:
115. Narrated Omar ibn Al-Khattab: 'The Messenger of
Allah said: "He that overslept and did not serve his Hizb
or a part of it and recited it between the time of performing
Al-Fajr and Ath-Thohr Prayers, it will be recorded for him as
if he had recited it by night."
Al Hizb, literally means party. It has two meanings in the Islamic
terms: a) One half of a Joz' of the Noble Koran. The Koran is
divided into thirty Joz's. b) What a Muslim abides to of good
deeds, i.e. reciting the Noble Koran, doing any form of
remembrance of Allah such as At-Tasbeeh, At-Tahmeed, At-
Tahleel, or doing good deeds such as performing a specific
number of Raka'as voluntarily, for the sake of Allah. This is what is
meant here.
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)

116. Narrated Abdullah ibn Amr ibn Al-A'as (may Allah
be pleased with them): 'Allah's Messenger said to me: "O
Abdullah! Do not be like so-and-so who used to offer (Al-
Tahjjod prayer) and then slackened from performing
it."(Bukhari)
*
